claim_boundary.allowed: sealed live solution packet for TDP-43 LCD mechanism language, pending external review
claim_boundary.not_allowed: universal protein-folding solved claim, TDP-43 atomic-coordinate solved claim, or FUS-specific grammar transfer claim

falsification_criteria: TDP-43 LCD residues 274-414 reproducibly adopt one compact single native fold under ordinary conditions
W/F/Y aromatic sticker mutations do not alter phase behavior or self-association
321-343 helix-prone segment perturbation has no effect on LLPS or ensemble signatures
nucleic-acid or multidomain context does not shift TDP-43 condensate material properties when binding/context is present
ALS-linked CTD mutations do not perturb LLPS or aggregation readouts in any reproducible direction
FUS-LC dense tyrosine-ladder grammar validates TDP-43 LCD predictions without TDP-specific evidence
independent holdouts contradict more than two explicit perturbation predictions

low_resolution_ensemble_prediction.ensemble_type: glycine-rich prion-like IDP ensemble with sparse-sticker LLPS and disorder-to-order maturation
low_resolution_ensemble_prediction.fibril_or_solid_boundary: state/context-dependent CTD ordering, not a whole-domain native fold
low_resolution_ensemble_prediction.state_model: soluble CTD disorder <-> reversible LLPS condensate <-> mutation/stress shifted amyloid or solid-like branch, modulated by RNA/DNA and phosphorylation/pathology state

operator_region_rationale.LLPS_self_association_operator: Low-complexity disorder plus sparse stickers predict concentration-sensitive condensate formation.
operator_region_rationale.alpha_helical_llps_segment_operator: The 321-343 window is enriched for hydrophobic, methionine, glutamine, asparagine, and sparse aromatic patterning.
operator_region_rationale.amyloid_or_solid_state_maturation_operat: The prion-like CTD should branch from liquid-like states toward amyloid/solid-like maturation under stress or mutation.
operator_region_rationale.disease_mutation_modulation_operator: UniProt disease variants concentrate in the CTD/LCD, predicting mutation-dependent phase/aggregation modulation.
operator_region_rationale.glycine_rich_prion_like_lcd_operator: The LCD is glycine-rich with Q/N-rich and low-complexity windows rather than a hydrophobic core.
operator_region_rationale.low_complexity_disorder_operator: Q13148 residues 274-414 overlap UniProt/DisProt disorder and low-complexity annotations.
operator_region_rationale.methionine_hydrophobic_cluster_operator: Methionines cluster at [{'position': 307, 'residue': 'M'}, {'position': 311, 'residue': 'M'}, {'position': 322, 'residue': 'M'}, {'position': 323, 'residue': 'M'}, {'position': 336, 'residue': 'M'}, {'position': 337, 'residue': 'M'}, {'position': 339, 'residue': 'M'}, {'position': 359, 'residue': 'M'}, {'position': 405, 'residue': 'M'}, {'position': 414, 'residue': 'M'}], predicting material-state and partner-sensitive modulation.
operator_region_rationale.nucleic_acid_context_switch_operator: TDP-43 is a nucleic-acid-binding protein; RNA/DNA context should shift CTD self-association and material properties.
operator_region_rationale.phosphorylation_pathology_shift_operator: UniProt annotates phosphoserine 292 and the LCD contains serines at [{'position': 292, 'residue': 'S'}, {'position': 305, 'residue': 'S'}, {'position': 317, 'residue': 'S'}, {'position': 332, 'residue': 'S'}, {'position': 333, 'residue': 'S'}, {'position': 342, 'residue': 'S'}, {'position': 347, 'residue': 'S'}, {'position': 350, 'residue': 'S'}, {'position': 369, 'residue': 'S'}, {'position': 373, 'residue': 'S'}, {'position': 375, 'residue': 'S'}, {'position': 377, 'residue': 'S'}, {'position': 379, 'residue': 'S'}, {'position': 387, 'residue': 'S'}, {'position': 389, 'residue': 'S'}, {'position': 393, 'residue': 'S'}, {'position': 395, 'residue': 'S'}, {'position': 403, 'residue': 'S'}, {'position': 404, 'residue': 'S'}, {'position': 407, 'residue': 'S'}, {'position': 409, 'residue': 'S'}, {'position': 410, 'residue': 'S'}].
operator_region_rationale.sparse_aromatic_sticker_operator: The LCD has sparse aromatic stickers at [{'position': 276, 'residue': 'F'}, {'position': 283, 'residue': 'F'}, {'position': 289, 'residue': 'F'}, {'position': 313, 'residue': 'F'}, {'position': 316, 'residue': 'F'}, {'position': 334, 'residue': 'W'}, {'position': 367, 'residue': 'F'}, {'position': 374, 'residue': 'Y'}, {'position': 385, 'residue': 'W'}, {'position': 397, 'residue': 'F'}, {'position': 401, 'residue': 'F'}, {'position': 412, 'residue': 'W'}], unlike the dense FUS-LC tyrosine ladder.

operator_regions.rationale: Q13148 residues 274-414 overlap UniProt/DisProt disorder and low-complexity annotations.
The LCD is glycine-rich with Q/N-rich and low-complexity windows rather than a hydrophobic core.

The LCD has sparse aromatic stickers at [{'position': 276, 'residue': 'F'}, {'position': 283, 'residue': 'F'}, {'position': 289, 'residue': 'F'}, {'position': 313, 'residue': 'F'}, {'position': 316, 'residue': 'F'}, {'position': 334, 'residue': 'W'}, {'position': 367, 'residue': 'F'}, {'position': 374, 'residue': 'Y'}, {'position': 385, 'residue': 'W'}, {'position': 397, 'residue': 'F'}, {'position': 401, 'residue': 'F'}, {'position': 412, 'residue': 'W'}], unlike the dense FUS-LC tyrosine ladder.

Methionines cluster at [{'position': 307, 'residue': 'M'}, {'position': 311, 'residue': 'M'}, {'position': 322, 'residue': 'M'}, {'position': 323, 'residue': 'M'}, {'position': 336, 'residue': 'M'}, {'position': 337, 'residue': 'M'}, {'position': 339, 'residue': 'M'}, {'position': 359, 'residue': 'M'}, {'position': 405, 'residue': 'M'}, {'position': 414, 'residue': 'M'}], predicting material-state and partner-sensitive modulation.
The 321-343 window is enriched for hydrophobic, methionine, glutamine, asparagine, and sparse aromatic patterning.

UniProt annotates phosphoserine 292 and the LCD contains serines at [{'position': 292, 'residue': 'S'}, {'position': 305, 'residue': 'S'}, {'position': 317, 'residue': 'S'}, {'position': 332, 'residue': 'S'}, {'position': 333, 'residue': 'S'}, {'position': 342, 'residue': 'S'}, {'position': 347, 'residue': 'S'}, {'position': 350, 'residue': 'S'}, {'position': 369, 'residue': 'S'}, {'position': 373, 'residue': 'S'}, {'position': 375, 'residue': 'S'}, {'position': 377, 'residue': 'S'}, {'position': 379, 'residue': 'S'}, {'position': 387, 'residue': 'S'}, {'position': 389, 'residue': 'S'}, {'position': 393, 'residue': 'S'}, {'position': 395, 'residue': 'S'}, {'position': 403, 'residue': 'S'}, {'position': 404, 'residue': 'S'}, {'position': 407, 'residue': 'S'}, {'position': 409, 'residue': 'S'}, {'position': 410, 'residue': 'S'}].
Low-complexity disorder plus sparse stickers predict concentration-sensitive condensate formation.
The prion-like CTD should branch from liquid-like states toward amyloid/solid-like maturation under stress or mutation.
TDP-43 is a nucleic-acid-binding protein; RNA/DNA context should shift CTD self-association and material properties.
UniProt disease variants concentrate in the CTD/LCD, predicting mutation-dependent phase/aggregation modulation.

perturbation_predictions.predicted_effect: weakens LLPS/self-association more than a generic composition-preserving shuffle

perturbation_predictions.perturbation: disrupt W334-centered and F-rich sticker neighborhoods
perturbation_predictions.predicted_effect: reduces condensate formation and changes local NMR/ensemble signatures

perturbation_predictions.perturbation: mutate or break the 321-343 helix-prone CTD segment
perturbation_predictions.predicted_effect: disrupts TDP-43-specific phase separation without requiring a FUS-like tyrosine ladder
collapses the TDP-43 LCD grammar and blocks the proper LLPS-to-aggregation state model
shifts phase behavior, aggregation, or interaction readouts in mutation-specific directions

perturbation_predictions.perturbation: increase phosphorylation or phosphomimetic charge in the CTD/LCD
perturbation_predictions.predicted_effect: alters condensate/aggregate/pathology readouts rather than creating one folded state

perturbation_predictions.perturbation: add RNA/DNA or restore multidomain nucleic-acid-binding context
perturbation_predictions.predicted_effect: rebalance CTD self-association, viscoelasticity, and aggregation tendency

perturbation_predictions.perturbation: isolate C-terminal fragments or extend stress exposure
perturbation_predictions.predicted_effect: increases amyloid/solid-like maturation relative to the soluble disordered CTD ensemble
must be rejected; the LCD is an ensemble/phase/prion-like transition problem

perturbation_predictions.perturbation: transfer FUS-LC dense tyrosine-ladder grammar onto TDP-43 LCD
perturbation_predictions.predicted_effect: must fail as an overfit transfer because TDP-43 uses sparse aromatics plus helix/prion-like CTD grammar

perturbation_predictions.perturbation: alter methionine-rich mid-CTD and C-terminal tail clusters
perturbation_predictions.predicted_effect: shifts material-state balance and partner/context sensitivity

predicted_context_switches.predicted_shift: increases CTD self-association and condensate formation

predicted_context_switches.context: temperature, salt, crowding, or hexanediol-like perturbation
predicted_context_switches.predicted_shift: shifts reversible liquid condensates versus aggregates

predicted_context_switches.context: RNA/DNA or multidomain TDP-43 context present
predicted_context_switches.predicted_shift: changes viscoelasticity and competes with CTD self-association

predicted_context_switches.context: CTD phosphorylation or phosphomimetic stress-pathology state
predicted_context_switches.predicted_shift: shifts aggregation/condensate readouts and pathology-associated material state
perturbs helix/aromatic/prion-like grammar and can bias solid-like maturation

predicted_state_grammar.grammar: heterogeneous glycine-rich prion-like ensemble without one native fold
few aromatic stickers and helix-prone CTD contacts nucleate condensates
disorder-to-order conversion emerges in selected CTD windows
ALS-linked CTD substitutions alter LLPS and aggregation balance
nucleic acid and folded-domain context rebalances CTD self-association

proposed_experimental_tests: mutate W/F/Y stickers, especially W334 and nearby F-rich neighborhoods, and measure turbidity, microscopy, FRAP, and NMR shifts
break the 321-343 helix-prone CTD segment and compare phase behavior to wild type
compare ALS-linked CTD mutants across LLPS, aggregation, and nucleic-acid-binding contexts
test phosphomimetic and phospho-null variants in CTD condensate and aggregate assays
add RNA/DNA or restore multidomain context and quantify viscosity, elasticity, recruitment, and self-association shifts
compare full CTD 274-414 to C-terminal fragments under aging/stress for amyloid or solid-like maturation
